mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Rank information coefficient between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.4%, the restrictive monetary policy stance elevates short-term discount rates and borrowing costs, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered weeks produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in nonfarm payrolls and decline in the unemployment rate signal a tightening labor market that drives wage income and consumption, producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns stronger than either series alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE level achieves a rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative industrial production growth during a Federal Reserve tightening cycle indicates that monetary restriction is constraining real output, amplifying earnings downside and producing negative S&P 500 monthly returns; this mechanism is absent during easing cycles.
failure_condition: In tightening cycles with negative INDPRO growth, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns occur more than 50 percent of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1250 thousand units signal weakness in residential investment that propagates to broader aggregate demand over a quarter, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered quarters produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate employment income, generating short-term positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets revise earnings expectations upward.
failure_condition: Rank information coefficient between PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising consumer prices and a high federal funds rate indicate tightening real monetary conditions that compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 monthly returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS level achieves a rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals slack in the labor market that depresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered months produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of walk-forward instances.
